sanskrit: त्वमादिदेवः पुरुषः पुराणस्त्वमस्य विश्वस्य परं निधानम् | वेत्तासि वेद्यं च परं च धाम त्वया ततं विश्वमनन्तरूप ||
transliteration: tvam ādi-devaḥ puruṣaḥ purāṇas tvam asya viśvasya paraṁ nidhānam | vettāsi vedyaṁ ca paraṁ ca dhāma tvayā tataṁ viśvam ananta-rūpa ||
literal_meaning: You are the primeval God, the Person, the Ancient; You are the supreme refuge of this universe; You are the knower and the knowable and the supreme abode; by You this endless-formed universe is pervaded.
contextual_meaning: Arjuna experiences a full surrender of ego-boundaries: the Self he thought was separate dissolves into the archetypal Ground of Being. All dualities—knower/known, past/future, inside/outside—collapse into one luminous field.
psychological_state: Peak self-transcendent experience; ego boundaries thin to transparency; sense of being held by an intelligence vaster than mind.
trigger_scenario: Watching a time-lapse of galaxies while on psychedelics; holding your first newborn at 3 a.m.; surviving a near-miss accident and feeling ‘seen’ by the universe.
anchor_text: Rest as the silent awareness in which all forms appear and dissolve.
